summary: Good soundboard recording captures a solid 1974 show highlighted by stellar China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and Scarlet Begonias, despite minor early mix hiccups.
review: The June‑22‑74 Charlie Miller SBD delivers clear stereo separation with the first few minutes of The Promised Land > Bertha a bit rough, but the balance quickly settles into an excellent full‑band sound. Musically the concert shines on China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, Scarlet Begonias, Playing in the Band and a soaring Greatest Story Ever Told, while a few mid‑set numbers like Jack Straw, Loose Lucy and Mexicali Blues fall short of the high bar.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently 4‑star reviews praising performance and sound quality; only minor mix issues noted, yielding a strong overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Great stereo separation and balanced mix after the opening few minutes; initial SBD mix glitches on The Promised Land > Bertha are corrected later, overall clear and well‑balanced A‑grade sound.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set takes a minute to build momentum then rides steadily; second set launches into long, exploratory jams that keep the energy rising through Playing in the Band and the Eyes‑Wharf‑Sugar medley.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Young, healthy Jerry praised for refreshing tone, guitar mini‑fans, noodles and smooth vocals on Deal.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass lines receive strong compliments; his solo bombs are noted, though one reviewer found a Phil solo in Eyes of the World a bit boring.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's organ/piano work is highlighted as stellar throughout (Me & Bobby McGee, Scarlet Begonias, etc.), with even a tongue‑in‑cheek cameo by Gloria Estefan on Scarlet.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming described as solid and well‑balanced across the set.
